description:
You are the mobile build engineer responsible for last night’s continuous-integration run.  
The raw CI log already exists on disk at  

 /home/user/build/logs/mobile_ci_2023-10-07.log  

Each relevant line inside that file has the exact shape (all upper-case keys, one space between fields):

[HH:MM:SS] MODULE=<module_name> PLATFORM=<platform> RESULT=<SUCCESS|FAILURE|WARNING> DURATION=<seconds>

Your job is to create a concise compliance summary for the managers.

Steps you must perform (≈ 5-10 shell commands total is plenty):

1. Make sure the directory /home/user/build/reports exists (create it if missing).

2. Parse /home/user/build/logs/mobile_ci_2023-10-07.log and calculate:
   a. TOTAL_MODULES  → total count of distinct MODULE entries.  
   b. SUCCESS     → number of lines whose RESULT is SUCCESS.  
   c. FAILURE     → number of lines whose RESULT is FAILURE.  
   d. WARNING     → number of lines whose RESULT is WARNING.  
   e. TOTAL_DURATION  → sum of all DURATION values (integer seconds).  
   f. An alphabetical list (one per line) of MODULE names whose RESULT is FAILURE.

3. Write the file /home/user/build/reports/ci_summary.txt in **exactly** the following line-oriented format (UNIX `\n` line endings, no trailing spaces, no extra blank lines). Replace the angle-bracketed numbers with the values you computed:

Line 1: TOTAL_MODULES=<integer>
Line 2: SUCCESS=<integer>
Line 3: FAILURE=<integer>
Line 4: WARNING=<integer>
Line 5: TOTAL_DURATION=<integer>
Line 6: FAILED_MODULES_START
Line 7+: <failed_module_1>
…
Last Line: FAILED_MODULES_END

If no modules failed, Line 6 **must** still appear and be followed immediately by `FAILED_MODULES_END` on the next line.

4. After creating the file, print its contents to STDOUT so the grader can see it.

The automated checker will verify that:

• /home/user/build/reports/ci_summary.txt exists.  
• Its contents strictly match the required format.  
• The numerical values and failed-module list are correct for the supplied log file.

You do **not** need to edit or move the original log.  Standard POSIX tools (`grep`, `awk`, `sort`, `wc`, etc.) are available.  Once the summary file is correct and displayed, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent starts:

1. Directory layout:
   /home/user/  
   └── build/  
       └── logs/  
           └── mobile_ci_2023-10-07.log

2. Contents of /home/user/build/logs/mobile_ci_2023-10-07.log (8 lines, each terminated with `\n`):

[00:01:12] MODULE=app_core PLATFORM=android RESULT=SUCCESS DURATION=120
[00:03:15] MODULE=app_ui PLATFORM=android RESULT=WARNING DURATION=60
[00:04:55] MODULE=networking PLATFORM=ios RESULT=FAILURE DURATION=45
[00:06:01] MODULE=database PLATFORM=android RESULT=SUCCESS DURATION=75
[00:07:40] MODULE=push_service PLATFORM=ios RESULT=FAILURE DURATION=30
[00:08:25] MODULE=analytics PLATFORM=android RESULT=SUCCESS DURATION=90
[00:10:55] MODULE=billing PLATFORM=ios RESULT=SUCCESS DURATION=110
[00:12:05] MODULE=crash_reporter PLATFORM=android RESULT=WARNING DURATION=55

3. The correct summary file that the agent must eventually create at /home/user/build/reports/ci_summary.txt:

TOTAL_MODULES=8
SUCCESS=4
FAILURE=2
WARNING=2
TOTAL_DURATION=585
FAILED_MODULES_START
networking
push_service
FAILED_MODULES_END

(Each line ends with `\n`; no additional blank lines.)  

Grading will read this file and compare it verbatim to the above block.
